rule_metadata.author: NTT Security - Axel Olsson, Patrik Olson
rule_metadata.title: Web Proxy Auto Discovery

rules.rule_text:
title: Web Proxy Auto Discovery
id: fd8657b5-ebcb-4e23-a383-3febd5bc3965
description: The Web Proxy Auto Discovery (WPAD) protocol attempts to auto-discover
  proxy settings and is used by e.g. web browsers to make it easier to get a working
  Internet connection. WPAD uses either the computer's domain name or DNS suffix (configured
  in Windows) or the domain name found in the DHCP lease (configured in the router/DHCP
  server) to look for a proxy configuration file 'wpad.dat' which is the same as a
  PAC file. If the host or router is configured with a domain that is not owned by
  the user/customer, then the owner of that domain can serve a malicious PAC file
  and intercept all HTTP traffic from the host (including HTTPS traffic if an HTTP
  downgrade attack is possible). Try to verify that the domain is registered and not
  parked, and examine the contents of the file if it exists.
status: stable
author: NTT Security - Axel Olsson, Patrik Olson
date: '2023-08-29'
modified: '2023-11-29'
references:
- https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Web_Proxy_Auto-Discovery_Protocol
- https://www.sentinelone.com/blog/in-the-wild-wpad-attack-how-threat-actors-abused-flawed-protocol-for-years/
logsource:
  category: proxy
detection:
  wpad:
    c-useragent: WinHttp-Autoproxy-Service/5.1
    c-uri: /wpad.dat
    cs-host|startswith: wpad
  internal_src:
  - vendor: Zscaler
  - src_ip: ''
  - src_ip|cidr:
    - 10.0.0.0/8
    - 192.168.0.0/16
    - 172.16.0.0/12
  filter:
  - cs-host:
    - wpad
  - cs-host|endswith:
    - .home
    - .localdomain
  condition: wpad and internal_src and not filter
falsepositives:
- Benign domains, create filters for customer-owned domains
level: low
tags:
- attack.collection
